opportunities.name: Settlement assurance + hold underwriting (acceptance with priced holds)

factors.basis: EV-2026-W28-004 (540-day hold, DFSA escalation), EV-2026-W28-005, EV-2026-W28-003
8 of 12 W28 records are hold/freeze/settlement pain across 7+ providers
EV-2026-W28-002/EV-2026-W28-008 support loops; EV-2026-W28-010 personal-account workarounds; multi-PSP redundancy costs integration + reconciliation
EV-2026-W28-011 explicit switch; EV-2026-W28-009 public escalations; hold events are inflection points by nature
mamo.md: merchants pay +0.5-0.75% for same-day settlement — priced, observed WTP for settlement speed
SEG-uae-online-sme-psp-merchants: online-acceptance merchants by definition
Acceptance volume bands unknown; needs segment sizing
EV-2026-W28-008 receivables gap; mamo.md paid-settlement uptake is a working-capital-demand proxy
BOTIM reach among online PSP merchants unknown; affected merchants are publicly identifiable, lowering CAC either way
As acquirer BOTIM sees full acceptance flow + chargebacks by construction — but adoption assumed
Acquiring margin real but competitive; net take after interchange paid away unknown
Hold advances are short-duration, episodic balances
Acquirer sees chargeback rates directly — the exact risk being underwritten; assumed pending build
Requires lender+acquirer combination PSPs lack (their handoff #1 thesis); Wio payments launch (IP-2026-001) bounds the window
Hold-affected merchants publicly identifiable on review platforms; interviews + waitlist feasible in weeks
Full acquiring stack (scheme membership, BIN sponsor) exceeds 7 weeks; only a concierge advance-against-existing-PSP-receivables variant fits

opportunities.hypothesis:
BOTIM acceptance (acquiring) with **guaranteed settlement timelines**, where risk reviews become **priced advances instead of frozen funds**: when a transaction pattern triggers review, AstraTech underwrites the chargeback exposure and releases the money for a fee, rather than holding it for weeks–months as UAE PSPs demonstrably do. Paid same-day settlement as a premium tier.

- **Segment:** `SEG-uae-online-sme-psp-merchants` (Workstream A definition) — plus marketplace sellers later (OPP-012).
- **Decision-maker:** owner (micro/small online merchants).
- **JTBD (evidenced):** "get the money my customers already paid me, when I was told I'd get it."

strongest_evidence.segment: SEG-uae-online-sme-psp-merchants (as bank customers); incorporated SMEs with board structures
strongest_evidence.title: Wio Business: accounts frozen 1–3+ months over routine ownership/director changes; inbound transfers rejected
Mamo: account suspended without notice, all funds held 540 days; merchant escalated to Sanadak and DFSA

strongest_evidence.segment: SEG-uae-online-sme-psp-merchants (micro end: freelancers, home businesses, small services)
strongest_evidence.title: Ziina: compliance-review holds run to months (vs stated 2–7 days); tail cases 45–120 days
Stripe UAE: account closed post-verification, ~$3.5K permanently withheld; slowest-baseline T+5 payouts
Amazon.ae sellers: disbursements missing or frozen in reserve for weeks–months, support loops without resolution

opportunities.validation_plan: Next action:** VE-003 (interviews + waitlist among hold-affected merchants), in parallel with the regulatory question.

score_history.subject: Ingest Workstream A handoffs: OPP-010 evidence-backed evaluation, OPP-011/012 candidates, VE-003

opportunities.next_action: VE-003 + regulatory review of settlement guarantees

opportunities.name: F&B weekend-cycle credit (sector-specific ultra-short revolving)
opportunities.classification_label: Weak (standalone) — fold into OPP-001 as segment config

factors.basis: Weekend stock crunch assumed acute for small F&B; needs REQ-002 evidence
Weekly cycle by construction
Bounded: one weekend's stock, not payroll or expansion
Produce suppliers often extend informal weekly terms; cost moderate
Same inflection points as OPP-001
MCA-style per-cycle fees tolerated in sector; unverified locally
Mixed; weekend receipts increasingly card/wallet
Receipts concentrated on weekends -> high routable share of flow
Recurring weekly but small-ticket
Same as OPP-001; F&B assumed well-represented on BOTIM
Weekend receipts give fast, dense repayment signal if routed
Wallet take only; balances small
Short duration + small balances -> low absolute revenue per merchant
5-day feedback loop; default visible within a week
Trivially copyable; is a parameterisation of OPP-001, not a moat
Same instruments as VE-001; could ride along in its interviews
Simpler than OPP-001 concierge: smaller limits, faster cycles

opportunities.hypothesis:
Ultra-short revolving credit for small F&B merchants matched to the weekly cash cycle: draw Thursday for weekend stock, weekend receipts arrive into the BOTIM wallet, repayment sweeps Monday. 4–7 day cycles, per-cycle fees (~0.5%/cycle ≈ 26% annualised), limits sized to one weekend's stock.

- **Segment (A):** F&B, 1–2 outlets, AED 60k–130k monthly revenue, UAE.
- **Organic switching reason:** credit timed exactly to the sector's cash rhythm; repayment invisible.
opportunities.validation_plan: Next action:** fold cycle-matched limit/sweep parameters into OPP-001's MVP design; no separate experiment warranted.

opportunities.name: Import payment + working-capital account (cross-border supplier T/T + cycle financing)
opportunities.classification_label: Promising but unvalidated (borderline Weak — switching_intent flag)

factors.basis: EV-2026-W28-013 (sev 3, fee/FX opacity), EV-2026-W28-015 (sev 4, financing cost), EV-2026-W28-014 (sev 3) — real but not existential
Per-shipment recurring; EV-2026-W28-013/014/015 frequency axes 3-4
EV-2026-W28-013 (AED ~105-130/transfer + 1.5-2.8% FX spread), EV-2026-W28-015 (LATR 8-15% p.a. or self-funding the 30-90d gap)
EV-2026-W28-013 provider-hopping, EV-2026-W28-014 escrow/wire adaptations, EV-2026-W28-015 LATR gated by collateral

EV-2026-W28-013/014/015 switching axes 3/2/2 — searching-for-alternatives on fees only; cheap rails already exist (Wio, Wise, exchange houses). Transfer-cost switching alone is weak; the credit must carry the wedge
Fees paid today (revealed, EV-2026-W28-013); LATR 8-15% paid at mechanism level (EV-2026-W28-015) — but UAE-importer-level WTP explicitly unverified in EV-015
Digital by trade necessity (platform escrow, T/T); segment marked mixed
Typical orders exceed card caps ($12-15k) per EV-2026-W28-014 — volume band per merchant unverified
Pay-upfront/collect-30-90d gap is the segment definition — but SEG-uae-importers-upfront-pay is Low confidence pending first-person accounts
BOTIM already operates consumer remittance corridors (incl. the rails EV-2026-W28-018 criticises); business-user overlap unknown
Sees supplier-payment flow if routed; goods-in-transit and money-in side invisible without account adoption
FX margin real but competed down (Wise from 0.31%, Wio AED 35-40) — transparent-pricing wedge caps the take
60-75 day cycles, real balances, priced against LATR 8-15% and MCA class (RC-4)
Outbound flow visible; repayment depends on money-in we may not see; goods-in-transit risk structural
IP-2026-002: CredibleX ($100M facility) and Comfi ($65M) deploying capital NOW; banks own LATR. BOTIM edge is below-the-floor reach + owned corridor rails - narrow
Importers reachable via trade communities; VE-004 doubles as Workstream A's segment-confidence upgrade instrument
Concierge feasible: manual FX transfer on existing BOTIM rails + manual financing on AstraTech licence — but EV-2026-W28-018 shows our own transfer execution must be fixed first

opportunities.hypothesis:
One account for small UAE importers: **pay overseas suppliers by T/T through BOTIM at transparent all-in FX pricing, and finance the pay-upfront/collect-30-90-days-later gap with AstraTech credit repaid from the sales cycle.** Financing is conditional on routing supplier payments through the account — routing *is* the underwriting data. Target: importers below/outside the AED 1M+ eligibility floor that funded fintechs (CredibleX, Comfi) serve.

- **Segment:** `SEG-uae-importers-upfront-pay` (Workstream A; **Low confidence — their stated upgrade condition is exactly what VE-004 collects**).
- **Evidence base:** EV-2026-W28-013 (AED ~105–130/transfer + 1.5–2.8% FX spread, provider-hopping), EV-014 (T/T is the rail — cards capped out of the core job at $12–15k/3%), EV-015 (financing mechanism validated by LATR at 8–15% p.a.; **UAE small-importer demand explicitly unverified**), EV-017/019 (competitor execution/compliance weakness), IP-2026-002 (funded competitors deploying now).

strongest_evidence.segment: SEG-uae-importers-upfront-pay (primary affected); more broadly UAE SMEs paying overseas counterparties via incumbent bank rails — **not all UAE SMEs**: cheaper rails exist and some merchants use them
strongest_evidence.title: Cross-border transfer fees and FX opacity push affected UAE SMEs into provider-hopping
Paid supplier-financing mechanisms exist in the UAE, but independent importer adoption and satisfaction remain unverified
Card rails appear unsuitable for many core supplier-payment transactions; cards retain a micro-order role

opportunities.validation_plan: Next action:** run VE-004; nothing else until it reports.

score_history.subject: Full-scale combined-agent test: OPP-013 import working-capital account from EV-013..019

opportunities.next_action: VE-004 (also A's segment-upgrade instrument); EV-018 rail fix is a hard pilot gate

feed.detail: MCA APR-equiv 40-350%, RBF 10-40%; 18-24% for revenue-linked sits well inside market acceptance (BENCHMARKS.md). Residual risk is bank competition for prime credits, not merchant acceptance

feed.title: Workstream A commits at least 10 evidence records to knowledge-base/customer-evidence/records/ by 2026-07-31

briefs.body:
# Meeting-Ready Recommendation — OPP-001 Revenue-Linked Revolving Credit

Issued 2026-07-10 per `opportunity-intelligence/templates/meeting-ready-output.md`. **Assumption-stage document:** evidence confidence is Low, so under this module's own rules the recommendation is capped at "run the experiment" — it is not a build recommendation.

---

## Page 1 — Decision view

### Recommendation
Approve validation experiment **VE-001** (3 weeks, ~15 interviews + 50 waitlist offers) now, with the 7-week concierge pilot pre-approved **conditionally** — it starts only if VE-001 passes its pre-committed threshold.

### The opportunity
- **Proposition:** an AstraTech working-capital line inside the BOTIM business wallet whose limit grows automatically with revenue the merchant routes in; repayment is a small share of incoming payments.
- **Segment:** F&B/retail owner-operators, 1–3 outlets, AED 50k–300k monthly revenue, UAE *(assumed — REQ-002 open)*.
- **Classification:** **Promising but unvalidated** — 15 of 17 scorecard dimensions are assumption-based, which caps the classification regardless of the composite (3.5 indicative).
- **Organic switching reason:** credit access unavailable elsewhere, with limits visibly linked to actual activity. Routing revenue *is* the credit application. No cashback or promotion anywhere in the design.
- **BOTIM advantage:** distribution to merchants already on BOTIM + real-time revenue visibility once flows route in. **AstraTech advantage:** lending licence, underwriting and collections capability — the loop needs both.

### The numbers (base case, downside in brackets; all inputs (A))
- Contribution margin: **+137 AED/merchant/month, 33%** (−59, loss-making)
- Break-even: **≈1,100 merchants** at base unit economics (never, in downside) — above the base month-12 count of 500; the model closes only with upside growth, higher routed share, or lower fixed cost
- Max free-credit days fundable from payment margin alone: ~24 (~3) — free days here are a lending-margin decision, not an interchange play
- VE-001 cost: staff time + incentives, ~3 weeks. Pilot cost if triggered: capped at 30 starter limits of AED 5k–15k (max exposure ≈ AED 450k) plus concierge ops

### What we are confident about vs not
| Confident (evidence/arithmetic) | Not confident (assumption) |
|---|---|
| The economics arithmetic itself; free-credit ceiling; that a 2%-cashback variant is unaffordable (OPP-003 rejected) | Pain severity/frequency for this segment in UAE; willingness to route ≥30% of revenue; ECL (6% base, 12% downside); achievable financing rate; organic CAC via BOTIM channel |

### Main invalidation risk
Merchants take the credit but won't move their *receiving* rails — routing decays or never starts (credit-and-run). This is the hardest behaviour change in payments, and it kills the data loop, the payment revenue, and the underwriting advantage simultaneously. Likelihood: genuinely unknown — which is exactly why VE-001 exists.

### The ask
1. Approve **VE-001** (3 weeks): pass = ≥40% of qualified merchants complete a waitlist that states the ≥30% routing condition; fail = ≤15% → OPP-001 is reclassified Weak and we stop.
2. Pre-approve the **7-week concierge pilot** conditional on VE-001 passing (scope, kill thresholds, and ≈AED 450k max credit exposure per the MVP plan).
3. Nudge Workstream A on **REQ-001/REQ-002** (evidence-ID scheme; working-capital pain evidence) so the scorecard can be re-based on evidence.

---

## Appendices (by reference — single source of truth)

- **A. Scorecard (all 17 scores):** `knowledge-base/opportunity-scores/opp-001-scorecard.json` (validate with `run.py score`)
- **B. Stress test & classification rationale:** `knowledge-base/product-ideas/opp-001-revenue-linked-credit.md` — strongest case against is the routing chicken-and-egg; adverse selection offset by small growing limits; cash-recycling fraud flagged
- **C. Commercial model (3 cases, break-even):** `knowledge-base/commercial-models/opp-001-computed.md` (engine-generated from `opp-001-inputs.json`; narrative in `opp-001-revenue-linked-credit.md`)
- **D. Value proposition & loop:** `knowledge-base/product-ideas/opp-001-revenue-linked-credit.md`
- **E. Validation plan (thresholds pre-committed):** `knowledge-base/validation/VE-001-revenue-routing-commitment.md`
- **F. Seven-week MVP (week-by-week, kill thresholds):** profile file, MVP section
- **G. Evidence register:** no evidence IDs consumed yet — none exist; open requests REQ-001..006 in `BACKLOG.md`

---

*Presentation rules honoured: ranges not point estimates; AED; Low evidence confidence → ask limited to running the experiment; classification applies to the proposition, not the company launch decision.*

OPP-010.name: Settlement assurance + hold underwriting
OPP-010.decision_banner: No product or build decision has been made.
OPP-010.note: Illustrative unit economics from committed model inputs. These are planning scenarios, not a forecast, and imply no build decision.

OPP-001.name: Revenue-linked revolving credit on BOTIM business wallet
OPP-001.decision_banner: No product or build decision has been made.
OPP-001.note: Illustrative unit economics from committed model inputs. These are planning scenarios, not a forecast, and imply no build decision.

OPP-009.name: F&B weekend-cycle credit
OPP-009.decision_banner: No product or build decision has been made.
OPP-009.note: Illustrative unit economics from committed model inputs. These are planning scenarios, not a forecast, and imply no build decision.

OPP-013.name: Import payment + working-capital account
OPP-013.decision_banner: No product or build decision has been made.
OPP-013.note: Illustrative unit economics from committed model inputs. These are planning scenarios, not a forecast, and imply no build decision.

experiments.title: Revenue-Routing Commitment for Revenue-Linked Credit
experiments.hypothesis: ≥40% of interviewed F&B/retail merchants (1–3 outlets) who report a working-capital gap in the last 90 days will complete a waitlist sign-up that requires them to state willingness to route ≥30% of incoming revenue through a BOTIM business wallet in exchange for a credit limit that grows with that routed revenue.
experiments.success_threshold: ≥40% of qualified merchants offered the waitlist complete it including the routing condition; AND ≥8 of 15 interviewees describe a costly current workaround (informal credit, personal cards, missed stock) without prompting.
experiments.kill_threshold: ≤15% waitlist completion, OR ≥8 of 15 interviewees show no costly workaround. Result between thresholds → inconclusive: redesign incentive or segment, do not extend silently.
experiments.method: two stages. - **Stage 1 — interviews (past behaviour first):** 15 structured interviews using the guide below. No product concept is shown until section C. - **Stage 2 — commitment proxy:** at interview end, and separately to a wider recruited sample, offer a real waitlist form. The form states the routing condition explicitly ("your limit grows with revenue received into the wallet; we expect at least ~30% of your incoming payments to flow through it") and requires trade-licence number and preferred monthly limit — a small but real cost to sign.
experiments.linked_opportunity: OPP-001 — Revenue-linked revolving credit on BOTIM business wallet (`opportunity-intelligence/test-cases/01-revenue-linked-revolving-credit.md`, backlog `BACKLOG.md`)
experiments.duration: 3 weeks from first interview; hard stop.
experiments.decision_informed: whether OPP-001 stays "Promising but unvalidated" and proceeds to a 7-week concierge MVP design, or is reclassified Weak (routing condition kills conversion). Decision owner: Workstream B, presented via meeting-ready output.

result.comment: Thresholds pre-committed from VE-001-revenue-routing-commitment.md BEFORE the run. Fill 'observed' after field work, then: python3 opportunity-intelligence/tools/run.py verdict knowledge-base/validation/VE-001-result.json

metrics.name: waitlist_completion_pct
metrics.description: % of qualified merchants offered the waitlist who complete it including the >=30% routing condition

metrics.name: costly_workaround_interviews
metrics.description: of 15 interviews: merchants describing a costly current workaround unprompted

metrics.name: no_costly_workaround_interviews
metrics.description: of 15 interviews: merchants showing no costly workaround (kill signal)

result.on_pass: OPP-001 stays Promising; start 7-week concierge MVP (conditionally pre-approved in recommendation 2026-07-10); re-score scorecard with interview evidence
result.on_fail: Reclassify OPP-001 as Weak in BACKLOG.md; run stress-test review before any archive decision
result.on_inconclusive: Redesign incentive or segment and re-run; do not extend the same design silently

experiments.title: Supplier-Payment Mapping and Card-Acceptance Willingness
experiments.hypothesis: ≥20% of the supplier relationships named by target merchants are payable by commercial card today or the supplier states willingness to accept card **without surcharging**, covering ≥30% of the merchants' monthly supplier spend by value.
experiments.success_threshold: ≥20% of mapped supplier relationships card-payable without surcharge AND those relationships cover ≥30% of supplier spend by value.
experiments.kill_threshold: <20% of relationships card-payable without surcharge → **reclassify OPP-002 as Weak** in the backlog. Between thresholds on the two conditions → inconclusive: extend supplier calls by 10 before deciding.
experiments.method: internal-data-free desk-plus-field mapping in two stages. - **Stage 1 — merchant interviews (12):** map each merchant's top 5 suppliers: instrument used (cash / transfer / cheque / card), payment terms (days), any surcharge experience, who sets the terms. Past behaviour only; no product concept shown — this experiment tests the rail, not the offer. - **Stage 2 — supplier calls (10):** for named suppliers: do you accept card today? At what cost to you (MDR awareness)? Would you accept card from a good customer without surcharge? What would make you refuse?
experiments.linked_opportunity: OPP-002 — Supplier-payment commercial card with free-credit days (`opportunity-intelligence/test-cases/02-supplier-payment-card.md`, backlog `BACKLOG.md`). Results also feed OPP-006 (inventory financing wallet).
experiments.decision_informed: whether OPP-002's commercial model gets built out to full `commercial-model.md` depth and proceeds toward MVP design, or is reclassified Weak with the acceptance data as the decisive record. Also sets the supplier-side feasibility input for OPP-006. Decision owner: Workstream B.

result.comment: Thresholds pre-committed from VE-002-supplier-payment-mapping.md BEFORE the run. Fill 'observed' after field work, then: python3 opportunity-intelligence/tools/run.py verdict knowledge-base/validation/VE-002-result.json

metrics.name: card_acceptance_share_pct
metrics.description: % of mapped supplier relationships card-payable without surcharge (today or stated willingness)

metrics.name: spend_coverage_pct
metrics.description: % of merchants' monthly supplier spend (by value) covered by those card-payable relationships

result.on_pass: Build OPP-002 full commercial model + MVP design; feed supplier-side feasibility into OPP-006
result.on_fail: Reclassify OPP-002 as Weak in BACKLOG.md with the acceptance data as the decisive record
result.on_inconclusive: Acceptance share passed but spend coverage below 30%: extend supplier calls by 10 before deciding, per spec

experiments.title: Hold-Underwriting Demand Test
experiments.hypothesis: ≥30% of contacted UAE online SME merchants who experienced a settlement hold or freeze of >14 days in the last 24 months will complete a waitlist for an acceptance product with guaranteed T+1 settlement where risk holds become priced advances (fee stated up front as 1–2% of the held amount).
experiments.success_threshold: ≥30% waitlist completion AND ≥7 of 12 interviewees describe the hold as a switching trigger unprompted.
experiments.kill_threshold: ≤10% waitlist completion, OR ≥7 of 12 interviewees operate in categories our own risk policy would hold (the wedge is a niche).
experiments.method: two stages. Stage 1 — 12 interviews (past behaviour first: what happened, what it cost, what they did next, whether they left the provider). Stage 2 — waitlist offer to ≥40 qualified merchants; the form states the pricing principle (holds become advances at a stated fee) and requires trade-licence number and current PSP. Non-leading rules per `opportunity-intelligence/templates/validation-experiment.md`.
experiments.decision_informed: whether OPP-010 proceeds to concierge advance-against-receivables pilot design plus the regulatory review, or is reclassified (Weak if the wedge is a high-risk niche). Decision owner: Workstream B via meeting-ready output.

result.comment: Thresholds pre-committed from VE-003-hold-underwriting-demand.md BEFORE the run. Fill 'observed' after field work, then: python3 opportunity-intelligence/tools/run.py verdict knowledge-base/validation/VE-003-result.json

metrics.name: waitlist_completion_pct
metrics.description: % of qualified hold-affected merchants completing the waitlist with pricing stated

metrics.name: switching_trigger_interviews
metrics.description: of 12 interviews: hold described as switching trigger unprompted

metrics.name: own_policy_would_hold_interviews
metrics.description: of 12 interviews: merchant in a category our own risk policy would also hold (kill signal)

result.on_pass: Design concierge advance-against-receivables pilot + start regulatory review of settlement guarantees; re-score OPP-010 with interview evidence
result.on_fail: Reclassify OPP-010 as Weak (niche wedge) in BACKLOG.md; hand verbatims to Workstream A
result.on_inconclusive: Between thresholds: extend waitlist offers by 20 before deciding; do not soften pricing disclosure

experiments.title: Importer Cycle-Financing Demand Test
experiments.hypothesis: ≥8 of 15 interviewed small UAE importers (below or outside the AED 1M fintech eligibility floor) confirm first-person the upfront-pay/collect-later working-capital gap, AND ≥25% of 40 offered accept a priced term sheet (≈1.5%/30 days, financing conditional on routing supplier payments through BOTIM).
experiments.success_threshold: ≥8/15 first-person gap confirmations AND ≥25% term-sheet acceptance.
experiments.kill_threshold: ≤4/15 gap confirmations (segment thesis fails — also fails Workstream A's upgrade condition), OR ≤8% term-sheet acceptance.
experiments.method: Stage 1 — 15 interviews, past behaviour first (last shipment: how paid, when collected, how the gap was funded, what that cost). Stage 2 — priced term-sheet offer to ≥40 qualified importers; pricing and the routing condition stated up front; acceptance = signed intent + trade-licence number.
experiments.linked_opportunity: OPP-013 (`../product-ideas/opp-013-import-payment-working-capital.md`). Doubles as the upgrade instrument for `SEG-uae-importers-upfront-pay` (its Low confidence lifts only on first-person importer accounts — verbatims hand back to Workstream A).
experiments.decision_informed: OPP-013 proceeds to concierge MVP design (manual FX transfer + manual financing) or is reclassified Weak; SEG confidence upgrade decision belongs to Workstream A on the same data.

result.comment: Thresholds pre-committed from VE-004-importer-cycle-financing.md BEFORE the run. Fill 'observed' after field work, then: python3 opportunity-intelligence/tools/run.py verdict knowledge-base/validation/VE-004-result.json

metrics.name: gap_confirmed_interviews
metrics.description: of 15 interviews: first-person confirmation of upfront-pay/collect-later gap

metrics.name: term_sheet_acceptance_pct
metrics.description: % of >=40 qualified importers accepting the priced, routing-conditional term sheet

result.on_pass: Design OPP-013 concierge MVP (manual FX transfer + manual financing on AstraTech licence, gated on EV-018 rail-quality fix); hand verbatims to Workstream A for SEG upgrade
result.on_fail: Reclassify OPP-013 as Weak in BACKLOG.md; if gap confirmations failed, flag SEG-uae-importers-upfront-pay thesis to Workstream A
result.on_inconclusive: One metric passed: extend offers by 20 or re-segment by corridor (China vs India vs Turkey) before deciding

predictions.statement: VE-001 reaches a conclusive verdict (pass or fail, not inconclusive) on its pre-committed thresholds by 2026-08-31
predictions.rationale: RC-8: waitlist conversions typically 5-15%; our 40/15 thresholds leave a wide inconclusive zone

predictions.statement: VE-001 passes: >=40% of qualified merchants complete the waitlist including the >=30% routing condition
predictions.rationale: RC-2 (10-30% sustain new rails) and RC-8 both argue the routing ask is hard; 0.25 reflects deliberate above-class threshold

predictions.statement: VE-002 finds >=20% of mapped supplier relationships card-payable without surcharge
predictions.rationale: RC-7 range 10-35%; UAE wholesale skews transfer-on-terms, so below class midpoint

predictions.statement: Desk research confirms AstraTech can price SME revolving credit at >=18% APR-equivalent without being uncompetitive
predictions.resolution_note: MCA APR-equiv 40-350%, RBF 10-40%; 18-24% for revenue-linked sits well inside market acceptance (BENCHMARKS.md). Residual risk is bank competition for prime credits, not merchant acceptance

predictions.statement: Workstream A commits at least 10 evidence records to knowledge-base/customer-evidence/records/ by 2026-07-31
VE-003 passes: >=30% waitlist completion among hold-affected merchants with pricing stated up front
predictions.rationale: RC-8 base rates argue low, but the recruited population is pre-qualified by a lived hold event (self-generating inflection point); priced disclosure pulls down

predictions.statement: VE-004 passes both thresholds (>=8/15 gap confirmations AND >=25% term-sheet acceptance)
predictions.rationale: SEG-uae-importers-upfront-pay is Low confidence (pulls down); IP-2026-002 funded-competitor existence implies real demand (pulls up); RC-4 supports the pricing

journal.note: Brier score over resolved, non-excluded predictions. Lower is better (0 = perfect, 0.25 = a coin flip at 50%).
